item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even with the revision with the containment specialist, I second this. This also feels like a very trodden path, with a familiar game coming together at a larger scale, such as SCP-5511, but instead of leaning into the goofiness, it's disappointingly subdued in a way. With these factors combined, I'm afraid this is a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The premise has merit, even though it's covering well-trod ground. I would've preferred to see the game play out without Foundation intervention, right now the reason who or whatever sent those balls through the tunnel isn't explored thoroughly enough to merit being part of the article, and it would be far more fun if the giant balls of granite caused some mayhem and destruction. This holds back on all the details that might make it fun and replaces them with the Foundation doing things that don't make sense and a pretty lousy 05 note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually did consider a test log of sorts, but I feared it might feel to similar to other skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd take this back to the drafts forum personally, but it's your choice. If you're aiming for a "lucky guess" feeling, maybe write several test logs outlining steps the Foundation took that failed first. Then after you've properly established that the Foundation has no idea how to contain the anomaly, perhaps they discover it accidentally?

E.G: The cargo helicopters are carrying the balls towards a nearby site, though the steel cable connecting the two snapped resulting in one of the balls falling through the small ring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose that's fair, but the "lucky guess" feeling was intentional. I tried to explain that anything else they could come up with was undesirable, which would validate the creative protocol they came up with, but ultimately it is just a guy saying "what if we drop the balls through the rings?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The issue here isn't the O5. It's the order itself.

At the suggestion of Containment Specialist Kenneth Alan, a course of action was agreed upon, and instructions were disseminated to containment staff. The agreed protocol involved dispatching several heavy-duty cargo helicopters to airlift and release the spherical objects from the entrance of SCP-5901 to the smallest and innermost ring hovering above SCP-5901.

There's nothing leading up to this. It's essentially still a random researcher saying "what if we drop the balls through the rings?". Nothing has hinted to the Foundation that this is what they need to do, so it all boils down to a lucky guess which leaves the article feeling incomplete.

Still -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since pretty much every comment has addressed the O5's in some way, I decided to edit them out. I also tweaked the Incident to make it seem like less of a leap in logic than before. Again, feedback welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, but it's a -1 from me.

The agreed protocol involved dispatching several heavy-duty cargo helicopters to airlift and release the spherical objects from the entrance of SCP-5901 to the smallest and innermost ring hovering above SCP-5901.

This order from the O5 is just way too on the nose and completely ruins the article, which is otherwise a pretty fun idea. It's just such a specific set of instruction that it ruins the suspension of disbelief when the O5 suggests it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta second Christopoulos here. The O5 order comes out of the left field and pretty much ruins any suspension of disbelief the SCP might've had at the beginning.

Besides that, we've had plenty of "this SCP actually resembles a simple IRL thing, but on a different scale" articles before, so I don't think showing them in a simple, straight-forward way works anymore. I was a bit hooked at the beginning, but the addendum came out of nowhere to tell me what the SCP is about. Admittedly, it made the ending kinda underwhelming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that O5 order doesn't really seem like something the O5 would do, but other than that great SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's my biggest gripe I have after writing this as well, but I felt I would be better off just posting rather than letting this collect dust.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty hard novote. As I said at some point during this article's draft stage, it seems like a non-sequitur for the O5 council to order them to throw one of the balls through the rings.

item.title: First Skip
item.content:encoded:
You think the O5's ever played skeeball?

Been meaning to post this for a while, but here it is. A big thanks to the SCPD discord for the crit and patience, you guys are the best. some of you are in need of a shower though.

Thanks to Christopoulos, Ultimate Ridley, TrustyOlValet2, and the rest of you critters from SCPD. Huge thanks to Mr.Glitchypants for the original idea.

Image is from Wikimedia Commons:

+ show block
– hide block

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Little_Road_Tunnel_on_Norway.jpg

Feedback and opinions are very welcome.
